texto: I'll admit that writing doesn't always come, but I'm totally against walking around looking at the sky when you're experiencing a block, waiting for inspiration to strike you. Tchaikovsky and Rimsky-Korsakov didn't like each other and agreed on very few things, but they were of one opinion on this: you had to write constantly. If you can't write a major work, write minor trifles. If you can't write at all, orchestrate something.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Dmitri Shostakovich: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a motivacao. O trecho “I'll admit that writing doesn't always come, but I'm totally against walking around looking at th…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que motivacao, composer, music pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de inspiracao e trabalho.

Tchaikovsky and Rimsky-Korsakov didn't like each other and agreed on very few things, but they were of one opinion on this: you had to write constantly. If you can't write a major work, write minor trifles. If you can't write at all, orchestrate something.

texto: When a man is in despair, it means that he still believes in something.
explicacao: Citação de Dmitri Shostakovich sobre temas ligados a familia. “When a man is in despair, it means that he still believes in something.” articula familia, despair, music em registro de familia, com relevância para contextos como music.
